title: Red Hat Security Advisory: image-builder security update
overview:
An update for image-builder is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: image-builder security update

An update for image-builder is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

An update for image-builder is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

Updated Packages:
image-builder-31-2.el9_7.src.rpm
image-builder-31-2.el9_7.x86_64.rpm
image-builder-debuginfo-31-2.el9_7.x86_64.rpm
image-builder-debugsource-31-2.el9_7.x86_64.rpm
image-builder-31-2.el9_7.s390x.rpm
image-builder-debuginfo-31-2.el9_7.s390x.rpm
image-builder-debugsource-31-2.el9_7.s390x.rpm
image-builder-31-2.el9_7.ppc64le.rpm
image-builder-debuginfo-31-2.el9_7.ppc64le.rpm
image-builder-debugsource-31-2.el9_7.ppc64le.rpm
image-builder-31-2.el9_7.aarch64.rpm
image-builder-debuginfo-31-2.el9_7.aarch64.rpm
image-builder-debugsource-31-2.el9_7.aarch64.rpm

CVE-2025-58183: A flaw was found in the archive/tar package in the Go standard library. tar.Reader does not set a maximum size on the number of sparse region data blocks in GNU tar pax 1.0 sparse files. A specially crafted tar archive with a pax header indicating a big number of sparse regions can cause a Go program to try to allocate a large amount of memory, causing an out-of-memory condition and resulting in a denial of service.
